message.content:
<teammate-message teammate_id="team-lead" summary="Task #5 done, CI green, need 9/9 consensus">
Task #5 (HTMX inline editing) committed, pushed, CI GREEN (be0e44a, 65 tests).

NOTE: Carson pushed before consensus — this was a process deviation from the updated flow. We'll address this in the final process update to TEAM_AGREEMENTS.md. For now, the code is pushed and CI is green.

ALL REVIEWERS: Please review commit be0e44a and send consent or concerns. This is the last task — we need 9/9 consensus.

Changes:
- New template: todo_item_edit.html (inline edit form fragment)
- Modified: todo_item.html (HTMX attributes on edit link)
- Modified: routes/todos.rs (new get_todo_item handler, HTMX fragment responses for edit)
- Modified: startup.rs (new GET /todos/{id} route)
- Modified: todo-focus.js (focus management for edit mode)
- 4 new integration tests, 65 total
</teammate-message>

content.text: This is the task I've been watching for -- it touches the route/service boundary. Let me do a thorough review.

input.description: Show files changed in Task #5 commit

content.content:

 templates/todo_item.html      |   1 +
 templates/todo_item_edit.html |  18 ++++
 tests/api/todos.rs            | 238 ++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++
 7 files changed, 401 insertions(+), 34 deletions(-)

1565 let client = 1566 register_and_login(&app.address, "htmx_edit@example.com", "securepassword123").await; 1567 1568 // Add a todo 1569 client 1570 .post(format!("{}/todos", &app.address)) 1571 .form(&[("title", "Inline edit me")]) 1572 .send() 1573 .await 1574 .expect("Failed to add todo"); 1575 1576 // Get the todo ID 1577 let response = client 1578 .get(format!("{}/todos", &app.address)) 1579 .send() 1580 .await 1581 .expect("Failed to get todos page"); 1582 let body = response.text().await.unwrap(); 1583 let todo_id = extract_todo_id(&body); 1584 1585 // GET edit with HX-Request header should return inline form fragment 1586 let response = client 1587 .get(format!("{}/todos/{}/edit", &app.address, todo_id)) 1588 .header("HX-Request", "true") 1589 .send() 1590 .await 1591 .expect("Failed to get inline edit form"); 1592 1593 assert_eq!( 1594 response.status().as_u16(), 1595 200, 1596 "HTMX edit should return 200" 1597 ); 1598 1599 let body = response.text().await.unwrap(); 1600 1601 // Should be a fragment, not a full page 1602 assert!( 1603 !body.contains(""), 1604 "HTMX response should be a fragment, not a full page" 1605 ); 1606 1607 // Should contain the inline edit form 1608 assert!( 1609 body.contains("todo-item--editing"), 1610 "Should contain the editing state class" 1611 ); 1612 assert!( 1613 body.contains("todo-item__edit-input"), 1614 "Should contain the edit input" 1615 ); 1616 assert!( 1617 body.contains("Inline edit me"), 1618 "Should contain the current title in the input" 1619 ); 1620 assert!(body.contains("Save"), "Should contain a Save button"); 1621 assert!(body.contains("Cancel"), "Should contain a Cancel button"); 1622 } 1623 1624 #[tokio::test] 1625 async fn htmx_post_edit_returns_updated_item_fragment() { 1626 let app = spawn_app().await; 1627 let client = register_and_login( 1628 &app.address, 1629 "htmx_edit_save@example.com", 1630 "securepassword123", 1631 ) 1632 .await; 1633 1634 // Add a todo 1635 client 1636 .post(format!("{}/todos", &app.address)) 1637 .form(&[("title", "Old HTMX title")]) 1638 .send() 1639 .await 1640 .expect("Failed to add todo"); 1641 1642 // Get the todo ID 1643 let response = client 1644 .get(format!("{}/todos", &app.address)) 1645 .send() 1646 .await 1647 .expect("Failed to get todos page"); 1648 let body = response.text().await.unwrap(); 1649 let todo_id = extract_todo_id(&body); 1650 1651 // POST edit with HX-Request header should return updated item fragment 1652 let response = client 1653 .post(format!("{}/todos/{}/edit", &app.address, todo_id)) 1654 .header("HX-Request", "true") 1655 .form(&[("title", "New HTMX title")]) 1656 .send() 1657 .await 1658 .expect("Failed to save inline edit"); 1659 1660 assert_eq!( 1661 response.status().as_u16(), 1662 200, 1663 "HTMX edit save should return 200" 1664 ); 1665 1666 // Should have announce trigger 1667 let hx_trigger = response 1668 .headers() 1669 .get("hx-trigger") 1670 .expect("HTMX edit response should include HX-Trigger header") 1671 .to_str() 1672 .unwrap(); 1673 assert!( 1674 hx_trigger.contains("announce"), 1675 "Should fire announce event, got: {hx_trigger}" 1676 ); 1677 assert!( 1678 hx_trigger.contains("updated"), 1679 "Announcement should mention update, got: {hx_trigger}" 1680 ); 1681 1682 let body = response.text().await.unwrap(); 1683 1684 // Should be a fragment, not a full page 1685 assert!( 1686 !body.contains(""), 1687 "HTMX response should be a fragment" 1688 ); 1689 1690 // Should contain the updated item (not the edit form) 1691 assert!( 1692 body.contains("New HTMX title"), 1693 "Should contain the updated title" 1694 ); 1695 assert!( 1696 !body.contains("todo-item--editing"), 1697 "Should not be in editing state after save" 1698 ); 1699 assert!( 1700 body.contains("todo-item"), 1701 "Should contain the todo-item class" 1702 ); 1703 } 1704 1705 #[tokio::test] 1706 async fn htmx_get_todo_item_returns_item_fragment() { 1707 let app = spawn_app().await; 1708 let client = 1709 register_and_login(&app.address, "htmx_cancel@example.com", "securepassword123").await; 1710 1711 // Add a todo 1712 client 1713 .post(format!("{}/todos", &app.address)) 1714 .form(&[("title", "Cancel edit me")]) 1715 .send() 1716 .await 1717 .expect("Failed to add todo"); 1718 1719 // Get the todo ID 1720 let response = client 1721 .get(format!("{}/todos", &app.address)) 1722 .send() 1723 .await 1724 .expect("Failed to get todos page"); 1725 let body = response.text().await.unwrap(); 1726 let todo_id = extract_todo_id(&body); 1727 1728 // GET /todos/{id} should return the item fragment (used by cancel button) 1729 let response = client 1730 .get(format!("{}/todos/{}", &app.address, todo_id)) 1731 .header("HX-Request", "true") 1732 .send() 1733 .await 1734 .expect("Failed to get todo item"); 1735 1736 assert_eq!( 1737 response.status().as_u16(), 1738 200, 1739 "GET /todos/{{id}} should return 200" 1740 ); 1741 1742 let body = response.text().await.unwrap(); 1743 1744 assert!(!body.contains(""), "Should be a fragment"); 1745 assert!( 1746 body.contains("Cancel edit me"), 1747 "Should contain the todo title" 1748 ); 1749 assert!( 1750 body.contains("todo-item"), 1751 "Should contain the todo-item element" 1752 ); 1753 assert!( 1754 !body.contains("todo-item--editing"), 1755 "Should be in view mode, not edit mode" 1756 ); 1757 } 1758 1759 #[tokio::test] 1760 async fn edit_link_has_htmx_attributes_for_inline_editing() { 1761 let app = spawn_app().await; 1762 let client = register_and_login( 1763 &app.address, 1764 "htmx_edit_attrs@example.com", 1765 "securepassword123", 1766 ) 1767 .await; 1768 1769 client 1770 .post(format!("{}/todos", &app.address)) 1771 .form(&[("title", "Check attrs")]) 1772 .send() 1773 .await 1774 .expect("Failed to add todo"); 1775 1776 let response = client 1777 .get(format!("{}/todos", &app.address)) 1778 .send() 1779 .await 1780 .expect("Failed to get todos page"); 1781 let body = response.text().await.unwrap(); 1782 1783 // The edit link should have hx-get for inline editing 1784 assert!( 1785 body.contains("hx-get"), 1786 "Edit link should have hx-get attribute for HTMX inline editing" 1787 ); 1788 assert!( 1789 body.contains("hx-target"), 1790 "Edit link should have hx-target for swapping the item" 1791 ); 1792 assert!( 1793 body.contains("hx-swap=\"outerHTML\""), 1794 "Edit link should swap outerHTML to replace the entire item" 1795 ); 1796 }

1757 } 1758 1759 #[tokio::test] 1760 async fn edit_link_has_htmx_attributes_for_inline_editing() { 1761 let app = spawn_app().await; 1762 let client = register_and_login( 1763 &app.address, 1764 "htmx_edit_attrs@example.com", 1765 "securepassword123", 1766 ) 1767 .await; 1768 1769 client 1770 .post(format!("{}/todos", &app.address)) 1771 .form(&[("title", "Check attrs")]) 1772 .send() 1773 .await 1774 .expect("Failed to add todo"); 1775 1776 let response = client 1777 .get(format!("{}/todos", &app.address)) 1778 .send() 1779 .await 1780 .expect("Failed to get todos page"); 1781 let body = response.text().await.unwrap(); 1782 1783 // The edit link should have hx-get for inline editing 1784 assert!( 1785 body.contains("hx-get"), 1786 "Edit link should have hx-get attribute for HTMX inline editing" 1787 ); 1788 assert!( 1789 body.contains("hx-target"), 1790 "Edit link should have hx-target for swapping the item" 1791 ); 1792 assert!( 1793 body.contains("hx-swap=\"outerHTML\""), 1794 "Edit link should swap outerHTML to replace the entire item" 1795 ); 1796 }

content.content:
354→/// Return a single todo item as an HTML fragment (for HTMX cancel-edit). 355→pub async fn get_todo_item( 356→ AuthenticatedUser(user_id): AuthenticatedUser, 357→ State(pool): State, 358→ Path(todo_id): Path, 359→) -> Result { 360→ let todo_item_id = TodoItemId::from_uuid(todo_id); 361→ 362→ let item = crate::infrastructure::todo_repository::find_todo_by_id(&pool, &todo_item_id) 363→ .await 364→ .map_err(|e| TodosError::Unexpected(anyhow::anyhow!("Database error: {e}")))?; 365→ 366→ match item { 367→ Some(todo) if todo.user_id() == &user_id => { 368→ let template = TodoItemTemplate { 369→ todo: TodoItemView::from(&todo), 370→ }; 371→ Ok(Html(template.render()?).into_response()) 372→ } 373→ Some(_) => Ok(( 374→ StatusCode::FORBIDDEN, 375→ Html("

Not authorized

".to_string()), 376→ ) 377→ .into_response()), 378→ None => Ok(( 379→ StatusCode::NOT_FOUND, 380→ Html("

Todo not found

".to_string()), 381→ ) 382→ .into_response()), 383→ } 384→} 385→ 386→#[derive(Debug, thiserror::Error)] 387→pub enum TodosError { 388→ #[error("Failed to render template")] Whenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. content.text: I have the full picture. Let me assess this from a domain architecture perspective and send my review.

input.content:
**I consent to Task #5 (be0e44a).**

This is the most significant change in this batch -- it adds a new route and modifies two existing handlers. From a domain architecture perspective:

**What's good:**

1. **No domain layer changes.** The `TodoTitle::parse()`, `update_todo_title` service, and `TodoItem` enum are all unchanged. The existing domain pipeline (validate -> execute -> persist) is reused intact. This is exactly how it should work -- the domain is stable and the presentation layer adapts around it.

2. **The new `get_todo_item` handler (routes/todos.rs:355-384) follows the established authorization pattern.** It loads the item, checks `todo.user_id() == &user_id`, and returns 403/404 for unauthorized/missing items. This is consistent with every other handler.

3. **The `TodoItemEditTemplate` view model is appropriately thin.** It carries `todo_id` and `current_title` as strings -- just what the template needs. No domain types leak into the template layer.

4. **Error handling in `post_edit_todo` correctly branches for HTMX.** On validation failure, it returns the inline edit form (not the full edit page) so the user stays in context. On success, it re-fetches the item and returns the `TodoItemTemplate` fragment. The domain error types (`UpdateTitleError::InvalidTitle`, `NotFound`, `Unauthorized`, `Unexpected`) are all still handled correctly.

**One observation (non-blocking):**

5. **`post_edit_todo` re-fetches the item after a successful `update_todo_title`** (line 296-300). This is because `update_todo_title` returns `()`, not the updated item. This is a minor inefficiency -- an extra DB query -- but it's the pragmatic choice given the current service signature, and it avoids changing the service contract just for a presentation concern. The re-fetch guarantees the returned fragment reflects the persisted state, which is actually the safest approach. I would not change this now.

6. **`get_todo_item` always returns a fragment** -- it doesn't check `is_htmx_request`. This is intentional since the route exists specifically for HTMX cancel-edit. A non-HTMX GET to `/todos/{id}` would return a bare `<li>` fragment without a page shell, which is unusual but not harmful. If we ever want a full-page single-item view, we'd add the HX-Request check then. Fine for now.

No domain concerns. The existing type-safe pipeline is preserved, authorization checks are consistent, and the new template/route follow established patterns. Ship it.
input.summary: Consent to Task #5 - domain pipeline preserved, clean
